rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 28, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. Defendant's verified Answer follows, paragraph by paragraph, Plaintiff's 395 paragraph Third Amended Complaint and either denies, admits, or denies based on lack of information or belief, each and every allegation. (Code Civ. Proc., section 431.30, subd. (d).) The denial in the answer puts the allegations directly at issue and makes it necessary for the plaintiff to introduce evidence thereof before he is entitled to judgment." (Cuneo v. Lawson (1928) 203 Cal. 190, 193-194; see also, Barasch v. Epstein (1957) 147 Cal.App.2d 439, 443 ["The denials of the answer are sufficient to, and do, create issues. Proof of the allegations of the complaint, which are denied, is necessary before plaintiff may recover judgment. Since the answer  denies material allegations of the complaint, it is sufficient as against a general demurrer and plaintiff was not entitled to judgment on the pleadings."].) While the Court agrees that Defendant's affirmative defenses are not well pled and consist of legal conclusions this does not undercut the fact that each material fact in the complaint has been denied and put at issue by the denial and therefore Plaintiff is not entitled to judgment on the pleadings. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL NAVONE VS. RUI XIA AN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 28, 2025 line 7. DEFENDANT RUI AN DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The First Amended Complaint states a cause of action under CCP 1161a. Specifically, plaintiff alleges that he purchased the premises through a trustee's sale where the premises were sold pursuant to Civil Code section 2924, and the allegations on the face of the pleading must be taken as true on demurrer. Plaintiff is not required to prove the truth of his allegations to withstand demurrer. Further, Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that a complaint under CCP 1161a must specifically plead compliance with Civil Code sections 2924m or 2924h, which are distinct from Civil Code section 2924 and are not referenced anywhere in CCP 1161a. Defendant's request for judicial notice of extrinsic information which has no bearing on the specific factual allegations of the First Amended Complaint is denied. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
